tactical_shape: created their own luck by taking the initiative from the opening whistle
kept pouring on the pressure after scoring
controlled the first half with half-space access and direct attacks up the channel
standout_players: Christian Pulisic — central to the early dominance, supplied the assist for McKennie and was a constant dribbling threat
Malik Tillman — lively in the first half, repeatedly nutmegged opponents and assisted Balogun's second goal
Weston McKennie — scored the opener and drove forward from midfield
Folarin Balogun — on World Cup debut, scored a 20-minute double
Tim Ream — started the move for the opening goal with a fizzed pass out from the back
Alex Freeman — involved in the build-up to the opener
Gio Reyna — late substitute, scored with the outside of his boot
fatigue_injuries: Christian Pulisic was taken off at half-time as a caution, rather than because of an injury
home_read.momentum: The US look buoyant and confident, having shown they can thrive under home-tournament pressure and play with purpose, though the second-half defensive issues are a reminder they still have faults.

tactical_shape: improved after the break by monitoring Balogun’s movement and tightening ranks
closed down the half-spaces that had been available to the US in the opening 45 minutes
standout_players: Orlando Gill — his restart created the turnover that led to Paraguay's goal
Miguel Almirón — quickly helped launch the move for the goal
Julio Enciso — provided the assist for Maurício's strike
away_read.momentum: Paraguay were outplayed for long spells but showed some resilience after half-time and at least found a consolation, though the opening defeat leaves them with defensive questions.
